Core Gameplay Loop.observations: The tap-to-spawn mechanic is physically intuitive — I can figure out that tapping does something. But I have no idea why I'm spawning cows or what winning looks like. The 'Graveyard' buttons are mysterious; I pressed one and my score dropped to zero, which felt punishing and confusing rather than strategic. There's no visible goal, no win condition, no timer. It feels like a tech demo, not a game loop.

UX / Usability.observations: The main menu is clean and familiar (Start, Load, Quit). But once I'm in the game, I'm lost. 'L: X' and 'R: X' look like a scoreboard but I don't know what I'm scoring or who the opponent is. The pause button is discoverable. The Graveyard buttons are labeled but their devastating effect (zeroing a score) is not communicated at all — I felt cheated the first time I hit one accidentally. No tutorial, no tooltip, no onboarding flow of any kind.

Visual Quality.observations: The 3D pastoral scene is genuinely appealing — grass, road, rolling hills, fences, sky, and a red car all read as a classic road trip setting. The cow and gravestone models are visually distinct. The mobile renderer produces a clean, colorful look that feels appropriate for the genre. This is clearly the most polished aspect of the build.

Game Feel.observations: Tapping and watching a cow pop into the scene gives a small hit of satisfaction. The spawning animation (if any) would add juice. The graveyard effect is dramatic but its meaning is opaque, so the emotional response is confusion rather than triumph or dread. There's no sound feedback mentioned, which would be a big gap on mobile.

Visual Correctness.observations: Everything rendered appears to be the right thing in the right place. Cows appear on the side you tap, gravestones appear when you press the Graveyard button. The score counters update correctly. No obvious visual glitches or misaligned UI elements reported. The 72pt score text is large and readable.

Performance Perception.observations: No stuttering, crashes, or loading delays were described. The scene and interactions feel responsive. Mobile renderer choice seems appropriate. Nothing in the described experience suggests performance issues.

top_strengths: The 3D pastoral road trip environment is charming and visually cohesive — immediately communicates the setting
Core tap interaction is physically intuitive and responsive
Main menu is clean and familiar, lowering the entry barrier
top_issues: Zero onboarding: there is no explanation of the rules, the scoring, or what 'Graveyard' does — a first-timer cannot understand the game without prior knowledge of the road trip tradition
The Graveyard button can silently destroy a score with no warning or confirmation, which feels broken rather than strategic to a new player
L/R score labels are completely unexplained — it's unclear if this is a solo game, a two-player game, or something else entirely
verdict: A visually appealing scene sitting on top of a completely unexplained game — without a tutorial or even a single line of rules text, a first-timer cannot understand what they are supposed to do or why.
